title: $28M SiriusXM TCPA Class Action Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who received more than one telemarketing call from SiriusXM within any 12-month period between April 2019 and October 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Received multiple telemarketing calls from SiriusXM
Calls occurred between April 2019 and October 2025
Calls were unsolicited or after opting out
summary: SiriusXM agreed to pay $28 million to settle claims it made illegal telemarketing calls. Class members may receive up to $5,000 depending on documentation.
caseInfo: Campbell v. SiriusXM Radio Inc., Case No. 2:22-cv-2261

title: $700M Equifax Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: U.S. consumers whose personal information was compromised in the 2017 Equifax data breach affecting approximately 147 million people.
eligibilityBullets: Had an Equifax credit file on or before September 2017
Personal information was compromised in the breach
U.S. resident at the time of the breach
summary: Equifax agreed to pay up to $700 million after a massive 2017 data breach exposed personal information of 147 million Americans.
caseInfo: In re Equifax Inc. Customer Data Security Breach Litigation, MDL No. 2800

title: $725M Meta (Facebook) Privacy Settlement
eligibility: Facebook users in the U.S. who had an active account between May 24, 2007 and December 22, 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Had a Facebook account during the class period
Account was active between May 2007 and December 2022
U.S. resident
summary: Meta agreed to pay $725 million to resolve claims that it allowed third parties to access users' personal data without consent.
caseInfo: In re Facebook Inc. Consumer Privacy User Profile Litigation, Case No. 3:18-md-02843

title: $3.4B Toyota Water Pump Defect Settlement
eligibility: Owners or lessees of certain 2013-2023 Toyota and Lexus hybrid vehicles with defective water pumps.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a qualifying Toyota or Lexus hybrid
Vehicle model years 2013-2023
Experienced water pump failure or related engine damage
proofDetails: Repair receipts or dealer service records showing water pump replacement or related engine damage.
summary: Toyota agreed to a $3.4 billion settlement over defective water pumps in hybrid vehicles that could cause engine failure.
caseInfo: In re Toyota Hybrid Water Pump Litigation, Case No. 8:20-cv-00629

title: $5B Google Incognito Mode Privacy Settlement
eligibility: Users who used Google Chrome's Incognito mode between June 2016 and the present while Google collected their browsing data.
eligibilityBullets: Used Google Chrome Incognito mode since June 2016
Google tracked browsing activity despite Incognito mode
No proof of damages required
summary: Google faces a $5 billion lawsuit for secretly collecting browsing data from users who believed Incognito mode kept their activity private.
caseInfo: Brown v. Google LLC, Case No. 5:20-cv-03664

title: $92M TikTok Biometric Privacy Settlement
eligibility: TikTok users in the U.S. who created or viewed videos on the platform before September 2021.
eligibilityBullets: Used TikTok in the United States
Created or viewed TikTok videos before September 2021
TikTok collected biometric data (face geometry) without consent
summary: TikTok agreed to pay $92 million to settle claims it collected facial recognition data and other biometric information without user consent.
caseInfo: In re TikTok Inc. Consumer Privacy Litigation, MDL No. 2948

title: $3.7B Wells Fargo Unauthorized Accounts Settlement
eligibility: Wells Fargo customers who had unauthorized accounts, credit cards, or insurance products opened in their name between 2002 and 2017.
eligibilityBullets: Were a Wells Fargo customer between 2002 and 2017
Had unauthorized accounts or products opened without consent
Suffered financial harm from unauthorized activity
summary: Wells Fargo agreed to pay $3.7 billion to settle claims that employees opened millions of unauthorized accounts and charged improper fees.
caseInfo: CFPB v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

title: $11B Roundup Weed Killer Cancer Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who used Roundup weed killer and were subsequently diagnosed with non-Hodgkin lymphoma or other cancers.
eligibilityBullets: Used Roundup weed killer products
Diagnosed with non-Hodgkin lymphoma or related cancer
Can document history of Roundup use
proofDetails: Medical records showing cancer diagnosis and evidence of Roundup usage (purchase records, employment records for agricultural workers).
summary: Bayer agreed to pay $11 billion to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits claiming Roundup weed killer caused cancer.
caseInfo: In re Roundup Products Liability Litigation, MDL No. 2741

title: $95M Apple Siri Privacy Settlement
eligibility: U.S. residents who owned or purchased a Siri-enabled Apple device (iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, Mac, HomePod, Apple TV) between September 17, 2014 and December 31, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Owned a Siri-enabled Apple device
Device was used between September 2014 and December 2024
Siri was activated (even accidentally) during this period
summary: Apple agreed to pay $95 million to settle claims that Siri recorded private conversations without user consent and shared them with third-party contractors.
caseInfo: Lopez v. Apple Inc., Case No. 4:19-cv-04577

title: $35M Snapchat Biometric Data Settlement
eligibility: Illinois residents who used Snapchat lenses or filters that scanned their face between November 2015 and the present.
eligibilityBullets: Illinois resident
Used Snapchat lenses or filters since November 2015
Snapchat scanned facial geometry without proper consent
summary: Snapchat agreed to pay $35 million to settle claims it violated Illinois' Biometric Information Privacy Act by collecting facial data through filters and lenses.
caseInfo: In re Snapchat Biometric Privacy Litigation

title: $190M Capital One Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who applied for or held a Capital One credit card or loan between 2005 and 2019 and were affected by the 2019 data breach.
eligibilityBullets: Applied for or held a Capital One product between 2005-2019
Personal information was exposed in the 2019 breach
Approximately 106 million people affected
summary: Capital One agreed to pay $190 million to settle claims stemming from a 2019 data breach that exposed personal information of over 106 million customers.
caseInfo: In re Capital One Consumer Data Security Breach Litigation, MDL No. 1:19-md-2915

title: $8.4M Uber Driver Misclassification Settlement
eligibility: Uber drivers in certain states who were classified as independent contractors instead of employees between 2013 and 2023.
eligibilityBullets: Drove for Uber between 2013 and 2023
Classified as independent contractor, not employee
Drivers in applicable states (NY, CA, MA, IL, others)
summary: Uber agreed to pay $8.4 million to settle claims that it misclassified drivers as independent contractors, denying them employee benefits.
caseInfo: O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc., Case No. 3:13-cv-03826

title: $30M Amazon Alexa Wiretapping Settlement
eligibility: U.S. residents who owned or used an Amazon Echo, Echo Dot, or other Alexa-enabled device that recorded conversations.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or used an Alexa-enabled device in the U.S.
Device recorded audio without explicit activation
Amazon stored and reviewed recordings
summary: Amazon agreed to pay $30 million to settle claims that Alexa devices illegally recorded and stored conversations without user consent.
caseInfo: In re Amazon Alexa Internet Privacy Litigation

title: $8.9B Johnson & Johnson Talc Cancer Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who used Johnson & Johnson talc-based products (Baby Powder, Shower to Shower) and were diagnosed with ovarian cancer or mesothelioma.
eligibilityBullets: Used J&J talc products (Baby Powder, Shower to Shower)
Diagnosed with ovarian cancer or mesothelioma
Can document product usage history
proofDetails: Medical records showing cancer diagnosis and documentation of talc product usage.
summary: Johnson & Johnson proposed an $8.9 billion settlement to resolve thousands of lawsuits claiming its talc products caused cancer.
caseInfo: In re Johnson & Johnson Talcum Powder Products Marketing, Sales Practices and Products Liability Litigation

title: $350M T-Mobile Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Current and former T-Mobile customers whose personal information was compromised in the August 2021 cyberattack.
eligibilityBullets: Were a T-Mobile customer affected by the August 2021 breach
Personal data (names, SSN, license info) was accessed
Approximately 76.6 million people affected
summary: T-Mobile agreed to pay $350 million to settle a class action lawsuit over a 2021 data breach that exposed personal data of 76.6 million customers.
caseInfo: In re T-Mobile Customer Data Security Breach Litigation, Case No. 4:21-md-03019

title: $5.6B Visa/Mastercard Swipe Fee Settlement
eligibility: U.S. merchants who accepted Visa or Mastercard payments between January 2004 and January 2019 and paid interchange fees.
eligibilityBullets: U.S. business that accepted Visa or Mastercard
Accepted payments between January 2004 and January 2019
Paid interchange or swipe fees on transactions
proofDetails: Merchant processing records showing Visa/Mastercard transactions and interchange fees paid.
summary: Visa and Mastercard agreed to pay $5.6 billion to settle claims they conspired to fix interchange fees charged to merchants.
caseInfo: In re Payment Card Interchange Fee and Merchant Discount Antitrust Litigation, MDL No. 1720

title: $150M Samsung Top-Load Washer Settlement
eligibility: Owners of certain Samsung top-load washing machines manufactured between 2011 and 2016 that were recalled due to excessive vibration and detachment of the top.
eligibilityBullets: Owned a Samsung top-load washer from the recalled models
Washer manufactured between 2011 and 2016
May have experienced lid detachment or excessive vibration
proofDetails: Proof of purchase or serial number of the affected Samsung washing machine model.
summary: Samsung agreed to pay $150 million to settle claims that certain top-load washing machines had a defect causing the top to detach during use.
caseInfo: In re Samsung Top-Load Washing Machine Marketing, Sales Practices and Products Liability Litigation

title: $3.1B Zantac Cancer Settlement
eligibility: Individuals who regularly took Zantac (ranitidine) and were diagnosed with cancer, particularly bladder, stomach, or esophageal cancer.
eligibilityBullets: Regularly used Zantac or generic ranitidine
Diagnosed with bladder, stomach, esophagal, or other cancers
Usage period typically before FDA recall in 2020
proofDetails: Medical records showing cancer diagnosis and proof of Zantac/ranitidine usage (prescription records, purchase history).
summary: Sanofi faces lawsuits claiming Zantac's active ingredient breaks down into a carcinogen, with a proposed $3.1 billion settlement fund.
caseInfo: In re Zantac (Ranitidine) Products Liability Litigation, MDL No. 2924

title: $100M Robinhood Trading Restrictions Settlement
eligibility: Robinhood users who held or attempted to trade GameStop (GME), AMC, or other restricted stocks during January 2021 when Robinhood limited trading.
eligibilityBullets: Had a Robinhood account in January 2021
Traded or attempted to trade restricted stocks (GME, AMC, etc.)
Suffered losses from trading restrictions
summary: Robinhood agreed to pay $100 million to settle claims it improperly restricted trading of GameStop and other meme stocks in January 2021.
caseInfo: In re Robinhood Financial LLC Trading Practices Litigation, MDL No. 3021

title: $65M Ford Explorer Exhaust Leak Settlement
eligibility: Owners or lessees of 2011-2019 Ford Explorer vehicles that experienced exhaust fumes leaking into the cabin.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased a 2011-2019 Ford Explorer
Experienced exhaust odors or carbon monoxide in the cabin
Vehicle was repaired or diagnosed with exhaust leak issue
proofDetails: Vehicle registration and repair records showing exhaust-related repairs or complaints.
summary: Ford agreed to pay $65 million to settle claims that 2011-2019 Explorer models had a defect allowing exhaust fumes to enter the cabin.
caseInfo: In re Ford Motor Co. Explorer Exhaust Litigation

title: $7.5M Spotify Data Privacy Settlement
eligibility: Spotify users in the U.S. who had a free or premium account between January 2018 and December 2023 and whose data was shared without adequate consent.
eligibilityBullets: Had a Spotify account (free or premium)
Account active between January 2018 and December 2023
Spotify shared listening data with third parties
summary: Spotify agreed to pay $7.5 million to settle claims it shared user listening data and personal information with advertisers without proper consent.
caseInfo: Doe v. Spotify AB

title: $25M Samsung Galaxy Throttling Settlement
eligibility: U.S. purchasers of certain Samsung Galaxy smartphones that were throttled through software updates to reduce performance.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased a qualifying Samsung Galaxy phone in the U.S.
Phone experienced performance throttling after updates
Models include Galaxy S20, S21, and Note series
summary: Samsung agreed to pay $25 million to settle claims it intentionally throttled Galaxy phone performance through software updates.
caseInfo: In re Samsung Galaxy Smartphone Performance Litigation

title: $345M EpiPen Overpricing Settlement
eligibility: U.S. consumers who purchased EpiPen or EpiPen Jr. auto-injectors between August 2011 and November 2020.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased EpiPen or EpiPen Jr. in the United States
Purchase made between August 2011 and November 2020
Paid out-of-pocket or through insurance co-pay
summary: Pfizer and Mylan agreed to pay $345 million to settle claims they conspired to overcharge consumers for EpiPen epinephrine auto-injectors.
caseInfo: In re EpiPen (Epinephrine Injection, USP) Marketing, Sales Practices and Antitrust Litigation

title: $46M Instacart Tip Skimming Settlement
eligibility: Instacart shoppers who completed deliveries between October 2016 and September 2020 and whose tips were used to subsidize base pay.
eligibilityBullets: Worked as an Instacart shopper
Completed deliveries between October 2016 and September 2020
Tips were used to offset rather than supplement base pay
summary: Instacart agreed to pay $46 million to settle claims it used customer tips to subsidize shoppers' base pay rather than paying tips on top of base earnings.
caseInfo: Gao v. Maplebear Inc. d/b/a Instacart

title: $115M Anthem Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Current and former Anthem health insurance members whose personal information was compromised in the 2015 data breach affecting 78.8 million records.
eligibilityBullets: Were an Anthem health plan member during the 2015 breach
Personal information (names, SSN, DOB, addresses) was exposed
Approximately 78.8 million people affected
summary: Anthem agreed to pay $115 million to settle claims stemming from a 2015 cyberattack that exposed personal data of nearly 79 million current and former members.
caseInfo: In re Anthem, Inc. Data Breach Litigation, Case No. 15-md-02617

title: $10M Keurig Recyclable K-Cup Pods Settlement
eligibility: U.S. consumers who purchased Keurig K-Cup pods labeled as 'recyclable' between 2020 and 2024, when many recycling facilities could not process them.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Keurig K-Cup pods in the United States
Pods were labeled or marketed as 'recyclable'
Purchased between 2020 and 2024
summary: Keurig agreed to pay $10 million to settle claims it falsely marketed K-Cup pods as recyclable when most recycling facilities could not process them.
caseInfo: Smith v. Keurig Dr Pepper Inc.

title: $6B 3M Military Earplug Settlement
eligibility: U.S. military service members and veterans who used 3M Combat Arms Earplugs (CAEv2) between 2003 and 2015 and suffered hearing loss or tinnitus.
eligibilityBullets: U.S. military service member or veteran
Used 3M Combat Arms Earplugs (CAEv2) between 2003-2015
Experienced hearing loss or tinnitus
proofDetails: Military service records and medical records documenting hearing loss or tinnitus diagnosis.
summary: 3M agreed to pay $6 billion to settle claims that its Combat Arms Earplugs were defectively designed, causing hearing loss in hundreds of thousands of service members.
caseInfo: In re 3M Combat Arms Earplug Products Liability Litigation, MDL No. 2885
